steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference in umami intensity: winged kelp has moderate umami while arame has higher umami. The correct choice must contrast these two intensities clearly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: mentions that winged kelp and arame both contain umami, then pivots to comparing ham and mushrooms — it groups the two seaweeds together rather than contrasting their intensity levels. ✗
  • Choice B: states that both seaweeds trigger umami responses, again treating them as equivalent rather than contrasting winged kelp's moderate umami with arame's higher umami. ✗
  • Choice C: explicitly states that arame's umami is more intense than winged kelp's, directly emphasizing the difference the student needs. ✓
  • Choice D: only notes that both winged kelp and arame are brown seaweeds — a botanical classification, not a comparison of umami intensities. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
